No. 13/13/2003/HP-l/Estt./228-230.—In exercise of the powers conferred by sub-sectSfe^^i Section 147 of the Delhi Police Act, 1978 (34 of 1978), the Lieutenant Governor ofthe National Ca^Rlf^Jrf^'^PBelSns pleased to make the following rules further to amend the Delhi Police (Appointment and Recruitment) Rules, 1980, namely:— 1. Short title and commencement (I) These rules may be called the Delhi Police (Appointment and Recruitment) (Amendment) Rules, 2014. Tyr* r * t~wr - w* ' (2) They snaN c o m e m t 0 f ° r c e w ^ effect fr°m the date of their publication in the v " * £ * « * * « * Delhi Gazette. 2, Amendment of IJui&nj&iv) In the Delhi Police (Appointment and Recruitment) Rules, 1980, in Rule 17-B (iv) iLiLJ?5 ^ l f ^ relating to the post of Assistant Wireless Operator/Tele-Printer Operator (H.C.) of • Communication Unit in Delhi Police. (aj r-'ort'Ke entry against column 4, relating to the 'Pay Band and Grade Pay/Pay Scale' the following entry shall be substituted, namely:— "Pay Band-1, Rs. 5200/- 20200/-, Grade Pay-Rs. 2400/-." (b) For the entry against the column 8, relatmg to the 'Educational and other Qualifications required for direct recruitment', the following entry shall be substituted, namely:— "(b) Proficientry in Computer Operation Qualifying in Nature. * Test English word processing speed—1000 key depressions in 15 minutes. * Text basic computer functions:— Opening/Closing of PC, Printing, MS office usage, saving & modification in typed text, paragraph setting & numbering etc. (c) For the entry against the column 11, relating to the method of Recuitment whether by direct recruitment or by promotion or by deputation/absorption and percentage ofthe vacancies to be filled by various methods, the following entry shall be substituted, namely:— " 100% by direct recruitment. (i) 90% vacancies shall be filled from the open competition and; (ii) 10% vacancies shall be filled from the Delhi Police confirmed Constables by giving them age relaxation as per GOIs instructions. Note: Entrance test for outside candidates and departmental candidates shall be same." By Order and in the Name ofthe Lieutenant Governor ofthe National Capital Territory of Delhi, G.P. SINGH, Add!. No. 13/13/2003/HP-l/Estt./228-230.—In exercise of the powers conferred ^ ^ D " s ® ^ n s y ^ ^ d ^ o f Section 147 oftheDelhi PoliceAct, 1978(34 of 1978), the Lieutenant Governor ofthe "National (Sigf^l^ffrofy^f'lBwi is pleased to make the following rules further to amend the Delhi Police (Appointment and Recruitment) Rules, 1980, namely:— 1. Short title and commencement (1) These rules may be called the Delhi Police (Appointment and Recruitment) (Amendment) Rule's, 2014. Amendmentq.f.KuI6=^B(iv) In the Delhi Police (Appointment and Recruitment) Rules, 1980, .in Rule 17-B (iv) *^^r*easT 'f*'*?$• relating to the post of Assistant Wireless Operator/Tele-Printer Operator (H.C.) of '" i^fe*; fa^ * Communication Unit in Delhi Police. (a) For the entry against column 4, relating to the 'Pay Band and Grade Pay/Pay Scale' the following entry shall be -. . substituted, namely:—. , • "Pay Band-1, Rs. 5200/- 20200/-, Grade Pay-Rs. 2400/-'.'1 (D). For the entry against the column 8, relating to the 'Educational and other Qualifications required for direct recruitment', the following entry shall be substituted, namely:— "(b) Proficientry in Computer Operation "Qualifying in Nature. . . .. .* TestEngiish word processing speed—1000 key depressions in 15.minutes. "* • Text basic computer functions:— * Opening/Closing of PC, Printing, MS office usage, saving & modification in typed text, paragraph setting & numbering etc. .•(c) For the entry against the column 11, relating to the method of Recuitment whether by direct recruitment or by promotion or by deputation/absorption and percentage of the vacancies to be filled by various methods, the following entry shall be substituted, namely:— "100% by direct recruitment. (i) 90% vacancies shall be filled from the open competition and; (ii) 10% vacancies shall be filled from theDelhi Police confirmed Constables by giving them age relaxation as per GOIs instructions. Note: Entrance test for outside candidates and departmental candidates shall be same." By Order and in the Name of the Lieutenant Governor ofthe National Capital Territory of Delhi, GP. SINGH, Addl.
